text: An {{c1::agent}} uses tools such as search, code execution, or databases to extend its capabilities beyond pure text generation.
explanation: Tool use is what turns a language model into an action-oriented system.
usage: Essential in copilots, workflow automation, and software engineering agents.
spanish: Un agente usa herramientas como búsqueda, ejecución de código o bases de datos para extender sus capacidades más allá de la generación de texto.

text: {{c1::Planning}} allows an agent to break a large goal into subtasks and choose an execution strategy.
explanation: Good planning improves reliability, especially for complex and multi-step tasks.
usage: Used in research assistants, coding agents, and orchestrated workflows.
spanish: La planificación permite que un agente divida una meta grande en subtareas y elija una estrategia de ejecución.

text: Reflection means the agent reviews its own {{c1::output}} and tries to improve or correct it.
explanation: Self-reflection can improve quality, though it also increases latency and cost.
usage: Useful in coding, research, and multi-pass document analysis.
spanish: La reflexión significa que el agente revisa su propio output e intenta mejorarlo o corregirlo.

text: Agent {{c1::memory}} stores important facts, preferences, and state across interactions.
explanation: Memory is what makes agents feel more personalized and persistent over time.
usage: Key for customer support agents, personal assistants, and long-running workflows.
spanish: La memoria del agente almacena hechos importantes, preferencias y estado a través de interacciones.

text: {{c1::Guardrails}} constrain agent behavior so it stays within policy, safety, and business rules.
explanation: They are a core part of making agents safe in production environments.
usage: Essential in finance, healthcare, and regulated enterprise scenarios.
spanish: Los guardrails restringen el comportamiento del agente para que se mantenga dentro de políticas, seguridad y reglas del negocio.

text: {{c1::Orchestration}} coordinates multiple steps, tools, and sub-agents to solve a broader task.
explanation: Orchestration is the system design layer that makes agent pipelines practical.
usage: Used in multi-step research, software engineering, and data analysis agents.
spanish: La orquestación coordina múltiples pasos, herramientas y subagentes para resolver una tarea más amplia.

text: Multi-{{c1::agent systems}} split work across specialized agents that coordinate around a shared objective.
explanation: This pattern can scale better for complex domains, but adds coordination and failure-mode complexity.
usage: Increasingly common in research, coding, and operations automation.
spanish: Los sistemas multiagente dividen el trabajo entre agentes especializados que coordinan alrededor de un objetivo compartido.

text: {{c1::Failure recovery}} lets an agent detect errors, retry, fall back, or ask for clarification when a tool or step fails.
explanation: Robust agents need graceful failure handling rather than brittle single-shot execution.
usage: Crucial in production automation and interactive assistant systems.
spanish: La recuperación de fallos permite que un agente detecte errores, reintente, haga fallback o pida aclaración cuando falla una herramienta o paso.
